title: Psychotria grandistipulata
taxonomy: Psychotria grandistipulata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This family consists of approximately 600 genera and over 13,000 species, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The Rubiaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse plant families in the world. Members of the Rubiaceae family are known for their often complex and pinnate leaves, as well as their showy flowers. Psychotria grandistipulata specifically is native to tropical regions and is often found in shaded, humid environments.

The plant has been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. Its leaves and stems are also used in traditional medicine to treat wounds and skin conditions.
